sanskrit:
प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च कार्याकार्ये भयाभये।
बन्धं मोक्षं च या वेत्ति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी॥
transliteration:
pravṛttiṃ ca nivṛttiṃ ca kāryākārye bhayābhaye |
bandhaṃ mokṣaṃ ca yā vetti buddhiḥ sā pārtha sāttvikī ||
literal_meaning: That intellect which correctly distinguishes between right and wrong action, between what is to be feared and what is not, between bondage and liberation, is sāttvic, O Pārtha.
contextual_meaning: A mind calibrated to reality sees the difference between healthy engagement and withdrawal, real versus imagined threats, and the subtle mechanics of psychological freedom versus entrapment.
psychological_state: Cognitive integration—thought, emotion, and value align, producing decisive yet flexible action.
trigger_scenario: A professional must choose between a lucrative but ethically gray project and a modest one aligned with personal values; the sāttvic intellect quickly senses which path nurtures long-term well-being.
anchor_text: See clearly what frees you and what keeps you stuck.
